rules per visibility:      \u2022 Public, PublicCoarse, OffchainOnly: caller must be subject or issuer        (matches AgentAssertion semantics).      \u2022 PrivateCommitment, PrivateZk: caller must The issuer        field still records who endorsed the commitment off-chain, but        the on-chain write is the holder's act."}},"title":"GeoClaimRegistry","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"mint(address,address,bytes32,uint64,bytes32,uint8,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,uint8,bytes32,uint64,uint64,bytes32)":{"notice":"Mint a geo claim."},"setEvidenceCommit(bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Re-anchor the evidence commitment (e.g. after a key rotation)."}},"notice":"Index over geographic claims an agent makes about a feature. This contract is small on purpose. It does NOT replace the public trust graph (`AgentRelationship`) or provenance (`AgentAssertion`) \u2014 public geo claims SHOULD also create an edge + assertion via the existing substrate, and reference them here via `edgeId` and `assertionId`. The point of the dedicated registry is:   \u2022 Versioned binding to a feature: a claim references a specific     (featureId, featureVersion). Boundary changes don't silently     re-target old claims.   \u2022 Visibility modes from day one \u2014 public, public-coarse, private-     commitment, private-zk, offchain-only \u2014 so we never end up with     exact addresses or receipts in SQL or on-chain metadata.   \u2022 An evidence commitment (`evidenceCommit`) that ZK proofs can later     verify against (e.g. the holder proves H3-membership-in-coverage     against this same commit).   \u2022 A relation enum so a verifier can discriminate \"lives there\" from     \"served a task there\" from \"is the steward of\" in a single query.   \u26a0 Locked-in policy id: `smart-agent.geo-overlap.v1`. Not used here     directly (scoring is off-chain), but the policy field on each claim     records which scoring policy it was minted under so future-policy     scores can be recomputed deterministically.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/GeoClaimRegistry.sol":"GeoClaimRegistry"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[":@openzeppelin/contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/",":account-abstraction/=lib/account-abstraction/contracts/",":erc4626-tests/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/erc4626-tests/",":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/",":halmos-cheatcodes/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/halmos-cheatcodes/src/",":openzeppelin-contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"src/AgentNameRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0xccbf1b04bdbba137f13f04f6d726cc908dff3a85b0d103b61f3f483af3b52aee","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://5cdb8e0eea33a437d0b2dd70a1af0e402d97b1f72b25c475eb02e9e8c0e92349","dweb:/ipfs/QmQkH6B9jF1H6rSxyGPMtjpc2NSkuPzudbPcMS5KrgpYWD"]},"src/AgentRelationship.sol":{"keccak256":"0x79063025928424d79229b22cb8c68783ac9ff3527cb5f2b2a92cf44aaf56a3bf","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://448c147db1a31c6526a37a05fe4be4dbd161984b2832e5ee552dc876c48c1762","dweb:/ipfs/QmYBgjKDKHRXrdLkvK7FZwisnfed4qnf764Atb3YXtmuW3"]},"src/GeoClaimRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x779dfa9309c35550e2d53d6bac0ae622692bdeb71458cf7682f543ec0d2fb03d","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://c52be2b939fc33c12ea3ba67b269e7b40ac771380c7f3345642c81816abea725","dweb:/ipfs/QmXy7x43dCfCrMgoryWjkn61it1k1xw8XPSVPpB1bjyz4h"]},"src/GeoFeatureRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x8010477e12013f9867f75ab3710b8b7fe267f652b18a54bef8bdee53e0a5e809","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://8db0bce52dd84cff48383aaccbe1da03c70d278dac0a8ecc2e0ca56780b085d2","dweb:/ipfs/QmUMCmrjbppyeG3iNRmE8883o3Vz1wCzg6ube8dKyszf1K"]}},"version":1}

mint(address,address,bytes32,uint64,byte.details: Authorization rules per visibility:      • Public, PublicCoarse, OffchainOnly: caller must be subject or issuer        (matches AgentAssertion semantics).      • PrivateCommitment, PrivateZk: caller must be subject. The issuer        field still records who endorsed the commitment off-chain, but        the on-chain write is the holder's act.

setEvidenceCommit(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Re-anchor the evidence commitment (e.g. after a key rotation).
